children.text: The National Park Service, under the direction of the Secretary of the Interior, shall promote and regulate the use of the Coronado National Memorial for the benefit and enjoyment of the people of the United States. Insofar as applicable and not in conflict with sections 450y to 450y–4 of this title, the Act of

children.tail: ), providing for the establishment of a National Park Service,
See References in Text note below.
as amended and supplemented, providing for the establishment of a National Park Service, shall govern the promotion and regulation of the designated memorial area:

, That nothing in sections 450y to 450y–4 of this title shall be construed to authorize any recreational or other development by the National Park Service within the sixty-foot strip north of the international boundary between the United States and Mexico withdrawn by proclamation of the President dated
(35 Stat., part II, p. 2136), unless such development has received the prior approval of the Secretary of State.
), providing for the establishment of a National Park Service, referred to in text, is
, known as the National Park Service Organic Act, which enacted sections 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title and provisions set out as a note under
, National Park Service and Related Programs. Sections 1 to 4 of the Act were repealed and restated as
, Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of Title 54 by
, 3260, 3272. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables. For disposition of former sections of this title, see Disposition Table preceding
, changed “Coronado International Memorial” to “Coronado National Memorial”.

children.text: For transfer of functions of other officers, employees, and agencies of Department of the Interior, with certain exceptions, to Secretary of the Interior, with power to delegate, see Reorg. Plan No. 3 of 1950, §§ 1, 2, eff.

children.tail: , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.
